protein_name: Transcriptional corepressor. Mediates the transcriptional repression activity of some nuclear receptors by promoting chromatin condensation, thus preventing access of the basal transcription. Isoform 1 and isoform 4 have different affinities for different nuclear receptors. Involved in the regulation BCL6-dependent of the germinal center (GC) reactions, mainly through the control of the GC B-cells proliferation and survival. Recruited by ZBTB7A to the androgen response elements/ARE on target genes, negatively regulates androgen receptor signaling and androgen-induced cell proliferation. Belongs to the N-CoR nuclear receptor corepressors family. Ubiquitous. High levels of expression are detected in lung, spleen and brain. 4 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
